channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Sofa and chair and other household waste dumped, 26th March
item.description:
This has been dumped today and there also is remaining waste that was not cleared away

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Rectory Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: NGP HMR lime bike outside 128 Cheviot garden, 26th March
item.description:
Dumped in street. Blocking pavement.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cheviot Gardens, Brent Cross, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping outside Churchward House, 26th March
item.description:
This afternoon someone left a large black duffel bag and a busted up radiator outside the entrance to Churchward House on Lorrimore Road. The duffel bag is full, not sure what the contents could be.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lorrimore Road, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Old fridge freezer that need collecting

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Swan Mead, Old Kent Road, Greater London

item.description:

item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag

item.title: 39 harvist road, 26th March
item.description:

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Harvist Road, London

item.title: Manhole cover serious trip hazard, 26th March
item.description:
As you can see from the picture and as previously said my blind registered friend fell down this, this should be dealt with urgently and it looks like no one‘s even looked at it yet. Surely someone can go and put a cone on it or something.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 38, Marion Crescent, Poverest, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Private waste fly tipped at recycling point, 26th March
item.description:
Overnight from 25/03/26 private rubbish was dumped at a recycling point which is only used for recycling. (In-between Longlands Convenience Store & block of flats). The rubbish contained food waste and disposable nappies so as a result of this overnight foxes have tore open the rubbish causing to be strewn across the path and road. This was discovered by me at 06:30 hrs on 26/03/26 and on returning home at 17:15 hrs it was still there. I have bagged up the rubbish and disposed of it but amongst the rubbish was pharmacy issued medication and a Sky TV statement in the name of a local resident which I have in my possession should you need it.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1-12, Main Road, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Chair and mattress, 26th March
item.description:
General house hold waste by Creasy Estate Cross from the care home

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Aberdour Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned Decaying Untaxed Vehicle dumped behind flats, 26th March
item.description:
The vehicle has been stationary and untouched for at least 4 years and has been reported by several residents due to mould growth and rat sightings. Also confirmed via DVLA as untaxed with no valid MOT certificate. Health Hazard: Significant mould growth is visible from the interior spreading outwards. Environmental Neglect: Flat tires and accumulation of debris/dirt and mold indicates it hasn't moved in years. Unroadworthy: The vehicle is no longer weather-sealed and is in a severe state of decay.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Vanbrugh Fields, Blackheath
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping blocking fire exit, 26th March
item.description:
There are lots of rubbish/fly tipping. This is a huge risk as it it blocking Greggs back fire exit passage.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bromley Road, Southend, Greater London
